titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Daniel Pinello Interviews on Same-sex Marriage
titlestmt.author: Camila Zorrilla Tessler

controlnote.p: This collection contains 95 published digital objects.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday January 5, 2024 at 08:25

did.unittitle: Daniel Pinello interviews on same-sex marriage
did.abstract: The Daniel Pinello interviews on same-sex marriage consist of oral histories that primarily focus on the effects of Super-DOMAS (Defense Against Marriage Acts). Super-DOMAs were in place in several states in the United States during the 1990s and 2000s and were overturned when same-sex marriage was federally legalized in 2015. These interviews were conducted by Daniel Pinello, a professor at John Jay College of Criminal Justice of the City University of New York, between 2004 and 2013. Pinello conducted the interviews in Ohio, Georgia, Michigan, and Texas, as part of his research for "America's War on Same-Sex Couples and their Familes And How the Courts Rescued Them," published in 2017.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open to research unless otherwise noted.
Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Public Services for further information.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Daniel R. Pinello graduated from Williams College with a B.A. in 1972, obtained a law degree from New York University in 1975 and a Ph.D. in political science from Yale University in 1991. He is a professor at John Jay College of Criminal Justice of the City University of New York and has written extensively on gay rights and the law.

The interviews in this collection stem from that focus, dealing especially with the impact on same-sex couples of Super-DOMA laws (Defense of Marriage Acts), or legislation that denied all rights of marriage to same-sex couples that existed in specific states previous to the federal right to same-sex marriage.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Daniel Pinello interviews on same-sex marriage consist of oral histories (recordings and transcripts) that primarily focus on the effects of Super-DOMAS (Defense Against Marriage Acts). Super-DOMAs were in place in several states in the United States during the 1990s and 2000s and were overturned when same-sex marriage was federally legalized in 2015. These interviews were conducted by Daniel Pinello, a professor at John Jay College of Criminal Justice of the City University of New York, between 2004 and 2013. Pinello conducted the interviews in Ohio, Georgia, Michigan, and Texas, as part of his research for "America's War on Same-Sex Couples and their Familes And How the Courts Rescued Them," published in 2017.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The Daniel Pinello interviews on same-sex marriage collection is arranged chronologically.

altformavail.head: Existence and Location of Copies
altformavail.p: The entire collection is available in digital form through the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.
